evaluation_protocol.one_shot_prompt_contract: The public spec exposes the supplied DUT, module interface, public stimulus coverage, save names, and Spectre syntax rules without revealing checker internals or private thresholds.
evaluation_protocol.feedback_track_note: A future feedback/debug track should reuse task assets only under a separate runner protocol and separate pass@K score table.

notes: Public prompt is now a thin task wrapper; public/agent_visible_spec.md is the shared agent-visible behavior contract.
Public spec removes the v1 public checker ID and private fraction thresholds.
The supplied DUT is copied into v2 public/support because this is a testbench-generation task.
Private checker policy is behavior-first; it does not require a fixed PWL breakpoint template.

private_diagnostics.private_threshold_policy: Resample vin/out, require below-window and above-window regions to stay low, and require inside-window rising/falling regions to stay high.
private_diagnostics.gold_expectation: The private gold testbench uses a 0 V to 0.9 V triangular PWL waveform over 90 ns and saves vin/out.

private_checker_contract.public_behavior_envelope: The public spec requires below-window, inside-window rising, above-window, and inside-window falling stimulus coverage with plain scalar vin/out saves.
private_checker_contract.implementation_constraints: No exact PWL breakpoint template is required by the v2 checker.

directive: This release task card is private benchmark/evaluator metadata. Do not render it into agent prompts.
